ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data source */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the geographical location */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Datetime of the weather report */,
  "dewpoint" REAL /* Dewpoint temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "wdir" INTEGER /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "qpf" REAL /* Quantitative Precipitation Forecast */,
  "pop" INTEGER /* Probability of precipitation */,
  "mslp" REAL /* Mean Sea Level Pressure in hPa */,
  "temp" REAL /* Air temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "sky" INTEGER /* Sky condition percentage */,
  "snow" REAL /* Snow accumulation in mm */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity percentage */,
  "wspd" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in meters */,
  "uv_index" INTEGER /* UV index for sun exposure levels */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES weather_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather source (e.g., wunder) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition (e.g., Fog, Overcast) */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather condition (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, analyst, general user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Datetime of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the report */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Datetime when the report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the report was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES weather_reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated weather report */,
  "generated_time" TEXT /* Datetime when the metadata was generated */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of report updates */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor used for data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_metadata_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES weather_reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City name */,
  "state" TEXT /* State name */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country name */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation in meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition instance */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated weather report */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated weather condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_conditions_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("condition_id") REFERENCES conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_conditions_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES weather_reports ("report_id")
)
